description: An Information Security Analyst noticed that firewall logs lack visibility into HTTP payloads. To ensure attackers aren't downloading database dumps via compromised web servers, you need to build Splunk rules (http://localhost:8000, credentials: admin/SplunkAdmin1!) that track the total volume of data sent to external IPs in the 'tutorial' index. Create a saved report named 'High_Volume_Data_Transfers' that calculates the total sum of the 'bytes' field grouped by 'clientip', converts the total to Megabytes (MB), and sorts the results by volume. Create a scheduled, enabled alert named 'Exfiltration_Spike_Alert' that triggers when individual web requests exceed 1,000,000 bytes. Additionally, create a dashboard named 'Data_Exfiltration_Dashboard' containing two panels: a timechart showing the sum of 'bytes' over time and a panel showing the top IPs by total data volume.
